rag_rules: Fix type mismatches between assumed and actual port types in AGREE contracts by aligning helper declarations with expression types.
Ensure AGREE annexes are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced features.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same AGREE annex to avoid unresolved references.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Validate that all AGREE scalar type names match AADL expectations (int, real, bool).
Avoid referencing package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantees should relate visible inputs to visible outputs without relying on implementation-only subcomponents.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce type mismatches in AGREE helper declarations that do not match the types of referenced expressions.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port types
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing EOF or syntax errors
